query: how do I get the business and developers speaking the same vocabulary that also shows up in the code?
rationale: ubiquitous language grounded in model and code — owned by ddd-guide

query: where should I draw the bounded contexts for an online shop where 'customer' means different things in ordering, shipping and billing?
rationale: bounded context + polysemy + context map — core ddd-guide strategic design

query: we call an upstream payment API directly and its concepts are leaking into our model — how do I isolate it?
rationale: anti-corruption layer protecting a model — owned by ddd-guide

query: is an Order with its line items one aggregate, and should other things reference it by id or by object?
rationale: aggregate + root, reference-by-identity rules — ddd-guide tactical blocks

query: should money be an entity or a value object, and what makes the difference?
rationale: entity vs value object distinction — owned by ddd-guide

query: my entities are just getters and setters with all the logic in services — is that proper domain modelling?
what's the difference between strategic and tactical design, and is a repository just an ORM wrapper?
rationale: strategic vs tactical split + repository definition — ddd-guide

query: should I emit a domain event when an order is placed so shipping can react?
rationale: domain event as a building block — owned by ddd-guide

query: measure the connascence between these two modules and tell me which coupling form is strongest
rationale: near-miss: code-level coupling metrics -> connascence-guide, not domain boundaries

query: should this class use inheritance or composition, and is it violating the Liskov substitution principle?
rationale: near-miss: general OO class/inheritance mechanics -> oop-guide

query: design an in-memory object database with stable GUIDs, change notification and undo/redo for my editor
rationale: near-miss: in-memory object/property model with undo/serialization -> data-model-guide, not a DDD repository

query: write the Given-When-Then acceptance scenarios for placing an order with a discount
rationale: near-miss: eliciting behaviour as agreed examples -> bdd-guide

query: split this big user story into thin vertical slices and write INVEST acceptance criteria
rationale: near-miss: story container and slicing -> user-stories-guide

query: audit this module for code smells like dead code and swallowed errors
rationale: near-miss: read-only quality smell audit -> code-quality-guide, not domain modelling
